calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Confirmirming Certain Assets Are Trust Property
Off Calendar Oct-12-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Michelle Raaka, via ZOOM, for C. Bradford Jeffries, Petitioner; David Parnall, via ZOOM, for Alison Regenold, Petitioner; Reed Harvey, via ZOOM, for Richard Bambery, Objector.

The court begins by querying Ms. Raaka regarding the issue of proper venue. Ms. Raaka confirms that her client's move to St. Helena is temporary and that his objective is to return to San Francisco. The court questions the principal place of administration at the time the petition was filed. Ms. Raaka reports that her clients' principal place of business is in San Francisco. The court then queries Mr. Harvey if he has had time to read Mr. Parnall's Reply. Mr. Harvey reports that his client has joined in Mr. Parnall's response which is included in his client's petition. Mr. Harvey then asks the court to remove the request for the court's consideration with regard to the Taplin property. The court confirms that the preceding is not before the court today. Mr. Parnall then confirms with the court that he is submitting with regard to the Summit property, only.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Order Confirming Certain Assets, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Order Confirmirming Certain Assets Are Trust Property
Off Calendar Dec-29-2020 Continued To Feb-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Michelle Raaka, Esq. for Petitioner, C. Bradford Jeffries

This matter comes on calendar for Petition for Order Confirming Certain Assets are Trust Property. The Court has reviewed all documents submitted. The Court believes there is more information needed. The Petition alleged that the principal place of administration for the trustee is San Francisco. The Court advises that the Trustee verified the petition. The Trustee was served in St. Helena. The supplement indicates that the Trustee moved between the time the petition was filed and the time he was served. The Court determines this may have happened within the matter of a month or so. The Court advises that this is conclusory. The Court is requesting more information about the move, which includes dates and details to base a decision on venue before the Court. Counsel to include a copy of the 16061.7 notes in the supplement, which is the notice that is sent out when a Trustee takes over. On the Taplin Road Property, the Court orders counsel to include a copy of the Recorded Deed of Trust. The Court orders this matter continued to February 16, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. Court stands adjourned.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Jones, Deputy: N. Ng. Matter Not reported.
